Phase-62.NAME: Compactness and Deletion Audit
Phase-62.objective: Remove, collapse, or compact bulky frontend surfaces after premium responsive adaptation.
Phase-62.non-goals: Removing required user/admin workflows, backend APIs, deploy/install scripts, or security controls.

steps.step-1: Inventory user, admin, public, and auth blocks that are duplicated, bulky, decorative, or low-value.
steps.step-2: Classify each candidate as remove, collapse, move secondary, compact, or keep.
steps.step-3: Apply user-cabinet compaction while preserving VPN, MTProto, subscription, devices, referrals, and settings.
steps.step-4: Apply admin-cockpit compaction while preserving operations, confirmations, analytics, and redaction.
steps.step-5: Apply public/auth compaction while preserving CTA, verification, reset, and tariff clarity.
steps.step-6: Create deletion-audit smoke markers for removed/collapsed surfaces and preserved workflows.
steps.step-7: Run user/admin builds, Phase-62 smoke, screenshots, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected surface guard.

Phase-62.acceptance: Frontend becomes less bulky while all primary workflows remain present and verifiable.
Phase-62.evidence: 2026-06-04: Phase-62 compactness/deletion audit folded secondary dashboard diagnostics, config import guidance, config diagnostics, subscription included/features copy, public value/features copy, MTProto alert archive/storage, and server legacy compatibility behind compact details surfaces. Required primary workflows, public/auth clarity, admin confirmations, redaction, bounded inventories, responsive markers, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed. Implementation commit 3d09fa8.
